steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content:
The sentence contains "sometimes characterized as idealizing" followed by "but in fact." The word "but" signals a direct reversal: the blank must express the opposite of idealizing human subjects. Look for a word meaning unfavorable, critical, or realistic in a negative sense.

Now check each choice against that requirement:
  • Choice A — 'unflattering': Unflattering means depicting someone in an unappealing or disadvantageous way — the direct antonym of idealizing. This fits the contrast perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'superficial': Superficial means lacking depth or thoroughness. A superficial depiction and an idealized depiction are independent qualities; a sculpture can be both shallow and flattering. Fails the contrast rule. ✗
  • Choice C — 'imprecise': Imprecise means not exact or accurate. Imprecision is about fidelity to reality, not about whether the subject is shown favorably. Fails the contrast rule. ✗
  • Choice D — 'implausible': Implausible means unconvincing or hard to believe. An implausible depiction says nothing about whether it flatters or criticizes the subject. Fails the contrast rule. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The "but" contrast requires a word that is the opposite of idealizing. Only "unflattering" directly opposes the concept of idealization. That matches choice A — A is correct.
